model.label: Claude Sonnet 4.6
model.description: Anthropic • Fast flagship Claude model for coding, analysis, and high-throughput agent use.

pick.rationale: Gonzaga enters as a massive favorite ranked #11 nationally by both KenPom and Torvik with an AdjEM of +28.16, a 9th-ranked defense (AdjDE 93.9), and a 30-3 record forged against WCC competition — a level of two-way excellence that Kennesaw State's Conference USA schedule (SOS ranked 184th nationally) never approached. The Owls qualified as a 14-seed by winning the CUSA Tournament with a 21-13 overall record, but their SRS of 0.13 (151st nationally), ORtg of 114.2, and DRtg of 104.2 reflect a mid-major profile that creates a roughly 28-point efficiency gap against the Bulldogs. The confirmed absence of star forward Braden Huff (dislocated kneecap, out since January 15) is a meaningful roster concern, but Gonzaga compiled their 30-3 record largely without him, demonstrating sufficient depth to absorb the loss against a Conference USA automatic qualifier. No credible upset path exists for Kennesaw State against this caliber of opponent, and the historical 3-vs-14 record and this specific efficiency chasm make Gonzaga's advancement a near-certainty.

reasoningStep.title: Gonzaga vs. Kennesaw State — Round of 64 Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Gonzaga is a dominant #11 national program facing a Conference USA automatic qualifier with a 21-13 record and a weak schedule. The efficiency gap is approximately 28 points. The only meaningful concern is the absence of Braden Huff (dislocated kneecap), but Gonzaga went 30-3 without him and has sufficient depth. Kennesaw State's offense (84th) and defense (112th) are both well below Gonzaga's caliber, and their SOS (184th) provides no preparation for this level of competition.
evidence: Gonzaga: KenPom #11, Torvik #11, AdjEM +28.16, AdjOE 122.1 (30th), AdjDE 93.9 (9th nationally), 30-3 record
Kennesaw State: 21-13 overall (6th in CUSA regular season), SRS 0.13 (151st), ORtg 114.2 (84th), DRtg 104.2 (112th), SOS -2.00 (184th) — no KenPom/Torvik ratings available
Braden Huff (Gonzaga star forward) confirmed OUT for the first weekend with a dislocated kneecap suffered January 15; Gonzaga went 30-3 without him demonstrating roster depth
Gonzaga is approximately an 18-20 point betting favorite, reflecting the massive efficiency and talent gap
Kennesaw State won the CUSA Tournament title for the first time in program history, their second-ever NCAA Tournament appearance in the Division I era

The March Madness Bracket is set, and first-round NCAA Tournament matchups are in place. It’s time to make your picks and predictions for the first round of the 2026 NCAA Tournament! We’re here to help as we’ll have picks and predictions for each of the first round 2026 NCAA Tournament games. Here are our NCAA Tournament picks and predictions for Gonzaga vs. Kennesaw State. NCAA Tournament Odds NCAA Tournament Pick’Em Contest NCAA Tournament March Madness Bracket Optimizer NCAA Tournament Betting Trends 2026 NCAA Tournament Picks & Predictions: Gonzaga vs. Kennesaw State Let’s dive into our top NCAA Tournament picks and predictions for this opening-round matchup. 2026 NCAA Tournament Preview: Gonzaga vs. Kennesaw State Here are the odds for this opening-round matchup of the 2026 NCAA Tournament. Let’s dive into our preview and predictions for this NCAA Tournament matchup. Gonzaga 2026 NCAA Tournament Preview Mark Few’s final season in the WCC has been far from a surprise. The Bulldogs won another WCC championship. Graham Ike has been as dominant as expected with 14 double-doubles, asserting himself as one of the best frontcourt players in college basketball. Braden Huff went down in January, but Few adapted, leaning into defense through Jalen Warley and Tyon Grant-Foster. The result is a top-10 defensive unit, both across the entire season and since January 15th, after Huff's injury. But there was a cost to losing Huff. Gonzaga’s offensive rating dropped to outside of the top 50, its three-point shooting fell to just over 30% and the Bulldogs dropped a Quad 3 game to Portland. What’s held everything together is Ike, who expanded his workload, raised his shooting efficiency and has been a ruthlessly efficient post scorer. Freshman Davis Fogle emerged as the wing rotation piece Few and his staff needed. Point guard Mario Saint-Supery is streaky but showed in the WCC championship against Santa Clara that he can be an off-ball threat from deep when Few creates looks for him. Gonzaga ranks in the top five nationally in both assist-to-turnover ratio and turnover margin. The Bulldogs won't beat themselves with the number of looks they get up every game, and with how much they limit second-chance opportunities and free buckets to their opponents. Gonzaga’s 27th straight tournament appearance is here. The last time Gonzaga

NCAA Tournament Odds NCAA Tournament Pick’Em Contest NCAA Tournament March Madness Bracket Optimizer NCAA Tournament Betting Trends 2026 NCAA Tournament Picks & Predictions: Gonzaga vs. Kennesaw State Let’s dive into our top NCAA Tournament picks and predictions for this opening-round matchup. 2026 NCAA Tournament Preview: Gonzaga vs. Kennesaw State Here are the odds for this opening-round matchup of the 2026 NCAA Tournament. Let’s dive into our preview and predictions for this NCAA Tournament matchup. Gonzaga 2026 NCAA Tournament Preview Mark Few’s final season in the WCC has been far from a surprise. The Bulldogs won another WCC championship. Graham Ike has been as dominant as expected with 14 double-doubles, asserting himself as one of the best frontcourt players in college basketball. Braden Huff went down in January, but Few adapted, leaning into defense through Jalen Warley and Tyon Grant-Foster. The result is a top-10 defensive unit, both across the entire season and since January 15th, after Huff's injury. But there was a cost to losing Huff. Gonzaga’s offensive rating dropped to outside of the top 50, its three-point shooting fell to just over 30% and the Bulldogs dropped a Quad 3 game to Portland. What’s held everything together is Ike, who expanded his workload, raised his shooting efficiency and has been a ruthlessly efficient post scorer. Freshman Davis Fogle emerged as the wing rotation piece Few and his staff needed. Point guard Mario Saint-Supery is streaky but showed in the WCC championship against Santa Clara that he can be an off-ball threat from deep when Few creates looks for him. Gonzaga ranks in the top five nationally in both assist-to-turnover ratio and turnover margin. The Bulldogs won't beat themselves with the number of looks they get up every game, and with how much they limit second-chance opportunities and free buckets to their opponents.
